posts.name: Jeremy Holmes
posts.cooked:
• 2013 Alain Hudelot-Noellat Vosne-Romanée 1er Cru Les Beaumonts - France, Burgundy, Côte de Nuits, Vosne-Romanée 1er Cru (7/11/2015)
    Popped into Bellota after the footy yesterday. A small stash of 2013 H-N had just lobbed and we had to hook into one in the name of science.

Our 2013 Hudelot-Noellat Vosne-Romanée ‘Beaumonts’ 1er Cru was fantastic. So much vibrancy, so much spice, so much deliciousness. Ripe red and black fruits are tinged with ginger and anise. It is plush and velvety in the mouth with great rocky detail. It really builds through the palate finishing with terrific cut.

Posted from CellarTracker

posts.name: Robert Panzer
posts.cooked:
Baby slayer, straight to the punch!
Sounds delicious, Jeremy.
I had a Hudelot Chambolle villages '13 last week that was blacker fruited than it seemed from barrel, but oh so very sexy good. Great verve and spice, like your Beaumonts impression. Charles did great in '13.
I also just polished off a Bertheau Chambolle Charmes '13 tonight. Great purity, and serious implosive broader-shouldered-than-not Charmes qualities. The last glass of day 3 was the best glass…it will age impeccably.

posts.name: Kent Comley
posts.cooked: Sounds great. Seems to me there has been an improvement in quality chez Hudelot Noellat with the last couple of vintages. I have found a few of the wines from mid 2000s to be a bit below par with some a tad Bretty.

posts.name: Ch@rlie F|_|
posts.cooked: been trying to get in a visit when I head over to burgundy early sept. No reply Love what Charles is doing with the wines. 12s were super from them, can’t wait to try their 13s. thx for the note

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: Pre-bottling the 2013s here seemed even better than the 2012s. It’s certainly a lovely vintage for this Domaine.

posts.name: alan weinberg
posts.cooked:
c fu:

been trying to get in a visit when I head over to burgundy early sept. No reply Love what Charles is doing with the wines. 12s were super from them, can’t wait to try their 13s. thx for the note

not such a great visit except that you can buy even the grand crus from them at the end, at decent pricing–at least that was the scene ten years ago.

posts.name: Robert Panzer
posts.cooked:
alan weinberg:
c fu:

been trying to get in a visit when I head over to burgundy early sept. No reply Love what Charles is doing with the wines. 12s were super from them, can’t wait to try their 13s. thx for the note

not such a great visit except that you can buy even the grand crus from them at the end, at decent pricing–at least that was the scene ten years ago.

A lot changes in ten years, guys.
Charles took over in full in '07. Hence, any bottles prior to that don’t reflect what is happening under his regime, other than the old vine raw materials that the estate is endowed with.
I think that the world is now fully banging down the Hudelot-Noellat door these days, and I would be highly doubtful that new private clients are taken on. I also am certain that the amount of requests that Charles gets for visits is, let’s just say, problematic (I don’t envy ANY grower who has to manage the sheer volume and passionate curiosity of the Burgundy fans of the world).
As to a visit in early September, Charlie, there is this little thing called harvest. Probably the worst time of year to try to have a grower set aside time for a taste and visit.

Charles took over in full in '07. Hence, any bottles prior to that don’t reflect what is happening under his regime, other than the old vine raw materials that the estate is endowed with.
I think that the world is now fully banging down the Hudelot-Noellat door these days, and I would be highly doubtful that new private clients are taken on. I also am certain that the amount of requests that Charles gets for visits is, let’s just say, problematic (I don’t envy ANY grower who has to manage the sheer volume and passionate curiosity of the Burgundy fans of the world).
As to a visit in early September, Charlie, there is this little thing called harvest. Probably the worst time of year to try to have a grower set aside time for a taste and visit.

I’m not going to be there during harvest. Unless harvest is starting three weeks before its original projections this year. I’m straddling August/sept.

posts.name: Robert Panzer
posts.cooked:
So is harvest, Charlie.
Late August, early September.
It has been HOT the past two weeks, pushing maturity along quickly.
Granted, things can change if they slow down remarkably, but not by that much at this point.
Yet another reason why folks can’t be very committal about visits that time of year. Things change.

posts.name: Ch@rlie F|_|
posts.cooked:
Robert Panzer:

So is harvest, Charlie.
Late August, early September.
It has been HOT the past two weeks, pushing maturity along quickly.
Granted, things can change if they slow down remarkably, but not by that much at this point.
Yet another reason why folks can’t be very committal about visits that time of year. Things change.

I know it’s not obvious since you feel the need to lecture me on harvest, but I’ve kept myself adequately appraised of when harvest may occur this year with ITB in burgundy. I’m not blindly planning a trip with my hands held out hoping for a drop of wine during potentially their busiest part of the season. Among all the domaines I’ve contacted, this is the only one without a response/appointment, so I’d think they seem to be okay with me visiting during this “worst time of the year”

Sorry for derailing your thread Jeremy. Keep up the good work.

posts.name: Robert Panzer
posts.cooked:
For somebody who doles out a LOT of smart alec, Charlie, you sure seem sensitive to taking a wee bit of ribbing. I know that you know better about harvest timing, and that you’re not new here. I was shooting for some irony.
Chive on.

Fwiw, Charles said that the first vintage that he was truly satisfied with, feeling like everything was in place and executed to his liking, was '12.
Additionally and somewhat surprisingly, the analyses of the '13s were higher in tannin, dry extract, and acidity than the '12s.
